L1.head: enzymatic activity
L1.claim: Enhanced enzymatic activity creates responsive sites

L1.head: responsive sites
L1.claim: Responsive sites interact with tumor biomarkers

L1.head: structural transformations
L1.claim: Structural changes modulate therapeutic and diagnostic outputs

L2.head: hierarchical defect sites
L2.claim: Defect sites expose iron centers with distinct coordination

L2.head: iron centers
L2.claim: Different coordination environments favor distinct enzymatic pathways

L2.head: external stimuli
L2.claim: Environmental stimuli trigger additional structural changes

L2.head: deligandation kinetics
L2.claim: Kinetics modulation enables programmable enzymatic behavior

L2.head: porous nanomaterials
L2.claim: Porous structure controls iron release kinetics

L2.head: iron release kinetics
L2.claim: Initial iron burst induces ferroptosis in cancer cells

L2.head: ferroptosis
L2.claim: Ferroptosis triggers DAMPs release from dying cells

L2.head: sustained iron release
L2.claim: Sustained iron enhances dendritic cell antigen processing

L3.head: contracted state
L3.claim: Contracted state minimizes toxicity in normal tissue

L3.head: expanded state
L3.claim: Expanded state exposes iron for Fenton chemistry

L3.head: structural changes
L3.claim: Structural changes create glutathione infiltration channels

L3.head: iron release
L3.claim: Iron release generates immunogenic lipid peroxidation

L3.head: lipid peroxidation products
L3.claim: Lipid products activate dendritic cells for immune response
